element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns control pad steering and acceleration before encountering hazards

element_introduction_order.element: Tune-up items (turbo acceleration, hotter engines, sticky tires)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Performance upgrades collected to improve vehicle capabilities; displayed on track conditions screen between races
Collecting all letters triggers car upgrade chain: truck → 4-wheeler → off-roader
Defensive collectible protecting from hazard damage

element_introduction_order.element: Hazards: oil slicks, water puddles, rain squalls
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Terrain obstacles that slow or spin out cars; learned through encounter
Offensive items collected as stars; carried over between races
Dynamic obstacles requiring evasion during active race
Penalty collectibles that force weapon conservation strategy

element_introduction_order.element: AI escalation: yellow car hyperacceleration (127 mph)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Late tracks (triggered by excessive weapon use)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Dynamic difficulty response; forces player to reconsider weapon spam tactics

typical_tension_triggers: Approaching track 24 (unofficial last track; largest/most complex)
Track 32+ repetitions with all AI at maximum speed
Weapon depletion against unbeatable AI opponents
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual escalation from safe mechanical learning (tracks 1-12) through hazard introduction and weapon gameplay (tracks 13-24) to survival-focused attrition phase (tracks 25+). Game has no formal end; players lose when weapons exhaust and cannot compete at maximum AI speed.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus letters scattered across tracks; collecting all letters triggers car upgrade—signals optional collection meta-game

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tracks 1-12: Introduction to steering, acceleration, and basic collectible items (tune-ups, letters). Safe mechanical learning phase with minimal hazard density.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Tracks 13-24: Hazard introduction (oil slicks, puddles, barriers) and weapon acquisition. Player learns to balance item collection with obstacle avoidance. Track 24 marked as largest/most complex configuration.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Tracks 25-32: Infinite loop of 12-track patterns with escalating features. Track 32 forces all AI opponents to maximum speed (127 mph, unbeatable without weapons). Game becomes attrition-based until player weapon supply exhausts.
notable_spikes: Track 25+ (feature accumulation: combines all previous hazards + escalated AI)
Track 32 (difficulty ceiling: AI speed becomes mathematically unbeatable)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each track forces top-3 placement requirement to progress. Failure results in game over (2 continues available, losing all points). Creates recurring tension/relief rhythm.
12 unique track configurations repeat indefinitely after track 24. Each repetition layer adds hazards and escalates AI behavior. Tracks 25-32 are repeats of 1-8 with additional features.
Collecting all bonus letters spelling NINTENDO triggers three-tier vehicle upgrade (truck → 4-wheeler → off-roader). Encourages optional collection meta-game.
Yellow opponent car automatically hyperaccelerates to 127 mph if player uses weapons excessively. Forces tactical weapon conservation in late game.
Game has no formal end. Players eventually exhaust ammunition reserves and cannot match AI maximum speed (track 32+), resulting in inevitable elimination.
